comment_text.sn35.248:2.1: This war goes on so long as day replaces night (SN 11.1:2.1).
comment_text.sn35.248:4.1: Such thoughts arise when the mind is perturbed; the same method applies to the following terms.
comment_text.sn35.248:7.1: A “state of conceit” (mānagata) is conceit that has been solidified into a view.

root_text.sn35.248:1.4: Evañhi sā, bhikkhave, yavakalāpī suhatā assa chahi byābhaṅgīhi haññamānā.
root_text.sn35.248:1.6: So taṁ yavakalāpiṁ sattamāya byābhaṅgiyā haneyya.
root_text.sn35.248:1.7: Evañhi sā bhikkhave, yavakalāpī suhatatarā assa, sattamāya byābhaṅgiyā haññamānā.
root_text.sn35.248:1.8: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano cakkhusmiṁ haññati manāpāmanāpehi rūpehi …pe…
root_text.sn35.248:1.11: Sace so, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano āyatiṁ punabbhavāya ceteti, evañhi so, bhikkhave, moghapuriso suhatataro hoti, seyyathāpi sā yavakalāpī sattamāya byābhaṅgiyā haññamānā.
root_text.sn35.248:2.2: Atha kho, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo asure āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.248:2.3: ‘sace, mārisā, devāsurasaṅgāme samupabyūḷhe asurā jineyyuṁ devā parājineyyuṁ, yena naṁ sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi bandhitvā mama santike āneyyātha asurapuran’ti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.4: Sakkopi kho, bhikkhave, devānamindo deve tāvatiṁse āmantesi:
root_text.sn35.248:2.5: ‘sace, mārisā, devāsurasaṅgāme samupabyūḷhe devā jineyyuṁ asurā parājineyyuṁ, yena naṁ vepacittiṁ asurindaṁ kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi bandhitvā mama santike āneyyātha sudhammaṁ devasabhan’ti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.6: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, saṅgāme devā jiniṁsu, asurā parājiniṁsu.
root_text.sn35.248:2.7: Atha kho, bhikkhave, devā tāvatiṁsā vepacittiṁ asurindaṁ kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi bandhitvā sakkassa devānamindassa santike ānesuṁ sudhammaṁ devasabhaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:2.8: Tatra sudaṁ, bhikkhave, vepacitti asurindo kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi baddho hoti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.9: Yadā kho, bhikkhave, vepacittissa asurindassa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn35.248:2.10: ‘dhammikā kho devā, adhammikā asurā, idheva dānāhaṁ devapuraṁ gacchāmī’ti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.11: Atha kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi muttaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati, dibbehi ca pañcahi kāmaguṇehi samappito samaṅgībhūto paricāreti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.12: Yadā ca kho, bhikkhave, vepacittissa asurindassa evaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn35.248:2.13: ‘dhammikā kho asurā, adhammikā devā, tattheva dānāhaṁ asurapuraṁ gamissāmī’ti.
root_text.sn35.248:2.14: atha kaṇṭhapañcamehi bandhanehi baddhaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati, dibbehi ca pañcahi kāmaguṇehi parihāyati.
root_text.sn35.248:2.17: Maññamāno kho, bhikkhave, baddho mārassa, amaññamāno mutto pāpimato.
root_text.sn35.248:3.1: ‘Asmī’ti, bhikkhave, maññitametaṁ, ‘ayamahamasmī’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘na bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘rūpī bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘arūpī bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘saññī bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘asaññī bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ, ‘nevasaññīnāsaññī bhavissan’ti maññitametaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:3.2: Maññitaṁ, bhikkhave, rogo, maññitaṁ gaṇḍo, maññitaṁ sallaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:4.1: ‘Asmī’ti, bhikkhave, iñjitametaṁ, ‘ayamahamasmī’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘na bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘rūpī bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘arūpī bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘saññī bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘asaññī bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ, ‘nevasaññīnāsaññī bhavissan’ti iñjitametaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:4.2: Iñjitaṁ, bhikkhave, rogo, iñjitaṁ gaṇḍo, iñjitaṁ sallaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:5.1: ‘Asmī’ti, bhikkhave, phanditametaṁ, ‘ayamahamasmī’ti phanditametaṁ, ‘bhavissan’ti …pe… ‘na bhavissan’ti … ‘rūpī bhavissan’ti … ‘arūpī bhavissan’ti … ‘saññī bhavissan’ti … ‘asaññī bhavissan’ti … ‘nevasaññīnāsaññī bhavissan’ti phanditametaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:5.2: Phanditaṁ, bhikkhave, rogo, phanditaṁ gaṇḍo, phanditaṁ sallaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:6.1: ‘Asmī’ti, bhikkhave, papañcitametaṁ, ‘ayamahamasmī’ti papañcitametaṁ, ‘bhavissan’ti …pe… ‘na bhavissan’ti … ‘rūpī bhavissan’ti … ‘arūpī bhavissan’ti … ‘saññī bhavissan’ti … ‘asaññī bhavissan’ti … ‘nevasaññīnāsaññī bhavissan’ti papañcitametaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:6.2: Papañcitaṁ, bhikkhave, rogo, papañcitaṁ gaṇḍo, papañcitaṁ sallaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:7.1: ‘Asmī’ti, bhikkhave, mānagatametaṁ, ‘ayamahamasmī’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘na bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘rūpī bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘arūpī bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘saññī bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘asaññī bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ, ‘nevasaññīnāsaññī bhavissan’ti mānagatametaṁ.
root_text.sn35.248:7.2: Mānagataṁ, bhikkhave, rogo, mānagataṁ gaṇḍo, mānagataṁ sallaṁ.

translation_text.sn35.248:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a sheaf of barley was placed at a crossroads.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.2: Then six people would come along carrying flails,
translation_text.sn35.248:1.3: and start threshing the sheaf of barley.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.4: So that sheaf of barley would be thoroughly threshed by those six flails.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.5: Then a seventh individual would come along carrying a flail,
translation_text.sn35.248:1.6: and they’d give the sheaf of barley a seventh threshing.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.7: So that sheaf of barley would be even more thoroughly threshed by that seventh flail.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.8: In the same way, an unlearned ordinary person is struck in the eye by both pleasant and unpleasant sights.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.9: They’re struck in the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.248:1.10: mind by both pleasant and unpleasant ideas.
translation_text.sn35.248:1.11: And if that unlearned ordinary person intends to be reborn in a new state of existence in the future, that futile person is even more thoroughly struck, like that sheaf of barley threshed by the seventh person.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.1: Once upon a time, a battle was fought between the gods and the titans.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.2: Then Vepacitti, lord of titans, addressed the titans,
translation_text.sn35.248:2.3: ‘My good fellows, if the titans defeat the gods in this battle, bind Sakka, the lord of gods, by his limbs and neck and bring him to my presence in the citadel of the titans.’
translation_text.sn35.248:2.4: Meanwhile, Sakka, lord of gods, addressed the gods of the thirty-three,
translation_text.sn35.248:2.5: ‘My good fellows, if the gods defeat the titans in this battle, bind Vepacitti by his limbs and neck and bring him to my presence in the Hall of Clear Right of the gods.’
translation_text.sn35.248:2.6: In that battle the gods won and the titans lost.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.7: So the gods of the thirty-three bound Vepacitti by his limbs and neck and brought him to Sakka’s presence in the Hall of Clear Right of the gods.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.8: And there Vepacitti remained bound by his limbs and neck.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.10: ‘It’s the gods who are principled, while the titans are unprincipled. Now I belong right here in the castle of the gods.’
translation_text.sn35.248:2.11: Then he found himself freed from the bonds on his limbs and neck. He entertained himself, supplied and provided with the five kinds of heavenly sensual stimulation.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.13: ‘It’s the titans who are principled, while the gods are unprincipled. Now I will go over there to the citadel of the titans,’
translation_text.sn35.248:2.14: he found himself bound by his limbs and neck, and the five kinds of heavenly sensual stimulation fell away.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.15: That’s how subtly Vepacitti was bound.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.16: But the bonds of Māra are even more subtle than that.
translation_text.sn35.248:2.17: When you conceive, you’re bound by Māra. Not conceiving, you’re free from the Wicked One.
translation_text.sn35.248:3.1: These are what is conceived: ‘I am’, ‘I am this’, ‘I will be’, ‘I will not be’, ‘I will have form’, ‘I will be formless’, ‘I will be percipient’, ‘I will be non-percipient’, ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient.’
translation_text.sn35.248:3.2: What is conceived is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.248:3.3: So mendicants, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will live with a heart that does not conceive.’
translation_text.sn35.248:4.1: These are what is perturbed: ‘I am’, ‘I am this’, ‘I will be’, ‘I will not be’, ‘I will have form’, ‘I will be formless’, ‘I will be percipient’, ‘I will be non-percipient’, ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient.’
translation_text.sn35.248:4.2: What is perturbed is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.248:4.3: So mendicants, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will live with a heart free of perturbation.’
translation_text.sn35.248:5.1: These are what is trembled: ‘I am’, ‘I am this’, ‘I will be’, ‘I will not be’, ‘I will have form’, ‘I will be formless’, ‘I will be percipient’, ‘I will be non-percipient’, ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient.’
translation_text.sn35.248:5.2: What is trembled is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.248:5.3: So mendicants, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will live with a heart free of trembling.’
translation_text.sn35.248:6.1: These are what is proliferated: ‘I am’, ‘I am this’, ‘I will be’, ‘I will not be’, ‘I will have form’, ‘I will be formless’, ‘I will be percipient’, ‘I will be non-percipient’, ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient.’
translation_text.sn35.248:6.2: What is proliferated is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.248:6.3: So mendicants, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will live with a heart free of proliferation.’
translation_text.sn35.248:7.1: These are states of conceit: ‘I am’, ‘I am this’, ‘I will be’, ‘I will not be’, ‘I will have form’, ‘I will be formless’, ‘I will be percipient’, ‘I will be non-percipient’, ‘I will be neither percipient nor non-percipient.’
translation_text.sn35.248:7.2: The state of conceit is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.248:7.3: So mendicants, you should train yourselves like this: ‘We will live with a heart that has struck down conceit.’”
translation_text.sn35.248:9.5: The Linked Discourses on the six sense fields are complete.
